LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. SPECIALLY-APPEARING DEFENDANT CLIVE ARMOUR'S AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. A MOTION TO QUASH IS NOT AN APPROPRIATE REMEDY FOR PROPER, ALBEIT UNTIMELY, SERVICE. SANCTIONS ARE INAPPROPRIATE AS DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE DELAY CAUSED HIM ANY PREJUDICE OR TO INCUR ANY ADDITIONAL EXPENSE. (OPPOSING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO SEND BY WAY OF E-MAIL TO THE COURT WITH A COPY TO THE PREVAILING PARTY.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF PAUL MATHEWS' MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(A)(1) TO FILE PLAINTIFF'S FIFTH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 473(A) ON ACCOUNT OF COUNSEL'S MISTAKE. ON MARCH 18, 2013, THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE DECLARATION OF CHOY, EXH. A.) IN PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S DECLARATION, HE STATES THAT HE INADVERTENTLY FILED THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT WITH INCORRECT ATTACHMENTS TO THE JUDICIAL COUNSEL PLEADING FORMS. (DECLARATION OF BRAVO IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR RELIEF, 3-4.) HOWEVER, THIS ORDER DOES NOT ADDRESS THE SUBSTANCE OF PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED FIFTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. ANY CHALLENGE TO THE PLEADINGS IN THE PLAINTIFF'S FIFTH AMENDED COMPLAINT SHOULD BE BROUGHT BY A PROPERLY FILED MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF PAUL MATHEWS' Motion For Relief Under Ccp 473(A)(1) To File Plaintiff'S Fifth Amended Complaint is granted pursuant to CCP 473(a) on account of counsel's mistake. On March 18, 2013, the Court granted Defendants' motion to strike punitive damages from the Third Amended Complaint, with leave to amend. (See Declaration of Choy, Exh. A.) In Plaintiff's counsel's declaration, he states that he inadvertently filed the Fourth Amended Complaint with incorrect attachments to the Judicial Counsel pleading forms. (Declaration of Bravo in Support of Motion for Relief, 3-4.) However, this order does not address the substance of Plaintiff's proposed Fifth Amended Complaint.
